mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, systematically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending forecasts, causing equity prices to decline over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65 percent of instances where the unemployment rate exceeds the 4.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the 3-month Treasury bill rate and the consumer price index compress household real purchasing power and corporate profit margins, driving negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion confirms resilient manufacturing output, supporting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of late-cycle periods following industrial production gains, or the signal shows no predictive power outside this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income and drives higher consumer demand, creating a persistent upward pressure on S&P 500 valuations over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 58 percent of cases, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.02 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.5 million units indicates a severe contraction in residential construction and related supply chains, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in over 62 percent of months where housing starts fall below the 1.5 million threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Declining inflation combined with a stable federal funds rate lowers real borrowing costs, expanding equity risk premiums and producing positive short-term market returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP during a monetary tightening cycle confirms economic contraction, prompting institutional deleveraging and sustained negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of quarters following negative GDP growth during tightening periods, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.
